summary: Iteration 2 — ABNDT Saved AI Chats: Continue/Share/Email + Phase 1-4 regression. Backend 11/11 PASS (saved conversations CRUD, public share endpoint, share invalid 404, auth requirement). Frontend: Admin /admin route flake from iter_1 is RESOLVED (login lands on /admin and stays). Continue this chat works end-to-end (navigates to /, panel opens, messages rehydrate, follow-up reply works). Public share viewer renders thread without auth, redirects only on invalid token (-> shared-chat-error). Anon /account/chats correctly redirects to /login?next=%2Faccount%2Fchats. Two test-env limitations to note (not real bugs): share button visual 'Link copied!' flip didn't trigger because navigator.clipboard.writeText is blocked in headless Playwright (network share call returned 200 + token); mailto: navigation couldn't be intercepted by window.location.href override, but the code path is correct. One real minor code issue: SavedChats.jsx has a delete <button> nested inside the row <button> -> invalid HTML & React hydration warning.

ui_bugs.issue: Nested interactive elements: <button data-testid='saved-chat-delete'> is rendered inside <button data-testid='saved-chat-item'>. Browser console logs a React hydration error ('In HTML, <button> cannot be a descendant of <button>'). Replace outer with a div + role='button' tabIndex=0, or move the delete button outside the row button (e.g., as a sibling positioned absolutely).

action_items: Fix nested <button> in /app/frontend/src/pages/SavedChats.jsx (lines ~154-170) — wrap row in <div role='button'> or move delete out of row.

Optional UX: when navigator.clipboard.writeText fails, still call setShareCopied(true) and show a toast so the 'Link copied!' affordance works in browsers with strict clipboard policies (currently the catch falls to window.prompt and skips the flip).
critical_code_review_comments: ai_routes.py public share endpoint correctly excludes _id projection and does not require auth dependency — verified by unauthenticated GET returning 200.
Share URL is built on the frontend from window.location.origin (SavedChats.jsx line 104) so backend APP_URL mismatch is harmless — good defensive design.
ConversationSaveRequest caps messages at last 50 — good. _derive_title fallback to first user message is sensible.
SharedChat.jsx uses raw axios (no Bearer header) deliberately — verified anonymous request works.
ai_routes.py is now ~410 lines; still under 700-line threshold but consider splitting saved-conversations into a separate router file as it grows.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all functional flows pass; share button label flip + mailto capture not verifiable in headless env but underlying behavior verified)

seed_data_creation: Created and then deleted one TEST_ conversation via API tests; one additional saved chat created via UI flow remains in admin's saved chats list and was shared with token ecb5d043f59b4ab192358caaf30e98b9 (used for public share verification).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend saved-chats + share endpoints fully verified (test_saved_chats_share.py). Frontend Continue/Share/Email flows verified end-to-end via Playwright on the public preview URL — including follow-up message after rehydration, anonymous public share viewer, and 404 error state for bad tokens. /admin route flake from iter_1 is resolved. The two outstanding items are: (1) cosmetic share button label flip when clipboard API is blocked, and (2) the nested <button> in SavedChats.jsx (hydration warning). No backend regressions; do not retest backend unless code changes.
rca of the issue: Nested button hydration warning RCA: in SavedChats.jsx the chat row is a <button> (line 154) with an inner <button data-testid='saved-chat-delete'> (line 162). HTML5 forbids interactive descendants of <button>, so React emits a hydration warning. Mitigation: change outer to <div role='button' tabIndex={0} onClick={...} onKeyDown={enter/space}> and keep inner button as-is; or absolute-position the delete button as a sibling of the row button.
